channel.title: Jacob's Research Journal
channel.description: Long-form notes on AI, technology, public systems, and the tools we use to think with. Written by Jacob Brooke in Jefferson City, MO.

item.title: Gemma 4 Is Google's Most Interesting Open Model Yet
item.author: jacob@example.com (Jacob Brooke)
item.description: Google's Gemma 4 family lands with a true Apache 2.0 license, a first-ever MoE architecture, audio support on edge models, and benchmarks that are competitive — if not quite dominant. Here's what it actually means.
item.content:encoded:
The thing that caught my eye about Gemma 4 wasn't a benchmark number. It was a license. Apache 2.0 — genuinely, with no asterisks, no carve-outs, no "harmful use" policy clause that gives Google a backdoor to revoke access if you become a competitor.

It dropped on April 2nd, 2026. Four models, across two different design philosophies, with technical depth that rewards reading the architecture paper.

Read the full post →
